hero.title: Our Story
hero.sub: More than just a cup of coffee

story.title: From Passion to Reality
story.p1: We started in 2026 in the heart of Baku with a small dream: we want to give people not just caffeine, but an inspiring experience. We believe every coffee bean tells a story – of the soil, the sun, and the hands that grew it.
story.p2: Our mission is to source the finest arabica beans directly from farmers, roast them using traditional methods, and present them to you.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At COFFEELAND, we respect your privacy and are committed to protecting your personal data. This policy explains how your information is collected and used when you use our website.
privacy.s1_text: We collect information such as your name, surname, email address, phone number, and delivery address to provide our services. Some technical data (IP address, browser type) may also be collected automatically during website navigation.
privacy.s2_title: 2. How do we use your information?
privacy.s2_text: The information we collect is used to process your orders, manage your account, improve our service quality, and, with your permission, inform you about new campaigns.
privacy.s3_text: Our website uses cookies to improve the user experience. You can disable cookies at any time from your browser settings.
privacy.s4_text: We use modern encryption methods and security protocols to ensure the security of your personal data.
privacy.s5_text: If you have any questions about our privacy policy, you can reach us at hello@coffeeland.az.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: Welcome to the COFFEELAND website and services. This document is a legal agreement governing the rules of use for all digital and physical services provided by us. By using this website, you are considered to have accepted all the terms and conditions set forth below unconditionally.
terms.s1_text: These Terms of Use constitute a formal agreement between COFFEELAND and the user, defining the rights and obligations of the parties. If you do not agree with any point of these terms, please stop using the website immediately. We reserve the right to unilaterally change these terms at any time without prior notice, and the updated terms shall be considered effective from the moment they are published on the site.
terms.s2_text: You are personally responsible for the accuracy and completeness of the information you provide when registering on our website. Keeping your account password confidential and all activities performed through your account are your direct obligation. If you suspect unauthorized access to your account by third parties, you are required to inform us immediately. COFFEELAND has the right to freeze or cancel the accounts of users who provide false or misleading information.
terms.s3_text: All content on the website, including logos, design elements, texts, graphics, videos, software, and other materials, belongs to the COFFEELAND platform and is protected by international copyright laws. Copying, transmitting, using for commercial purposes, or modifying these materials without our written permission is strictly prohibited. Any illegal use discovered will result in appropriate legal action.
terms.s4_text: Each order placed through the website is considered a formal offer and is deemed accepted once confirmed by us. Product prices and availability are subject to change at any time. Payments must be made using only the provided secure methods. Delivery times and conditions may vary depending on the chosen address and logistical capabilities. COFFEELAND is not responsible for delays caused by force majeure events (natural disasters, governmental decisions, etc.).
terms.s5_text: We do not guarantee that the website will operate 24/7 without interruption or errors, although we mobilize all our technical capabilities for this purpose. In no event shall COFFEELAND be liable for any direct or indirect losses, data loss, or damage to devices that may occur during the use of the site by the user. All risks arising from non-compliance with the terms of use fall on the user.

blogs.title: Blogs from the Coffee World
blogs.sub: You are in the right place to learn what is happening in the world of coffee.
blogs.author: Author

items.title: Secrets of the Perfect V60 Brew at Home
items.excerpt: Learn all the intricacies of V60 brewing, from the right grind size to water temperature.
items.author: David Miller
items.content: Brewing coffee is a science, and V60 is the most elegant part of this science. Many people overlook some important details when making coffee at home. First of all, water quality is decisive. Filtered water reveals the taste of coffee more clearly. Secondly, the water temperature should be 92-94 degrees. Boiling water can burn the coffee. Thirdly, the grind size of the coffee is important. A medium grind, like sea salt, is ideal for V60.

items.title: Our New Cold Drinks for the Summer Season
items.excerpt: Meet our iced coffee menu specially prepared to cool off in hot weather.
items.author: Sarah Jones
items.content: As the COFFEELAND team, we celebrate the arrival of spring with new and refreshing drinks. This year, the 'Blueberry Cold Brew' and 'Honey Lavender Iced Latte' we added to our menu have already become our customers' favorites. Our coffees prepared with the cold brew method are brewed for 24 hours at room temperature, resulting in a low-acid and sweet taste profile drink.

items.title: Ethiopia: Journey to the Home of Coffee
items.excerpt: Get to know the rich coffee culture of the lands where the Arabica bean was discovered.
items.author: Alex Rivera
items.content: Ethiopia is the cradle of the coffee world. According to legend, a shepherd named Kaldi discovered coffee while observing that his goats became energetic after eating the fruits of a certain tree. Today, each region in Ethiopia (Yirgacheffe, Sidamo, Harrar) has its own unique taste profile. Ethiopian coffees are generally known for their floral and fruity notes.

items.title: History of Espresso: From Italy to the World
items.excerpt: Discover how fast coffee turned into a piece of art.
items.author: Marco Rossi
items.content: Espresso means 'under pressure'. Originating in Milan in the early 20th century, this method is the coffee industry's contribution to the industrial revolution. The first machines worked with steam pressure, but the modern 9-bar pressure standard was introduced by Achille Gaggia in the 1940s.

items.title: How to Choose the Right Coffee Bean?
items.excerpt: Our guide to different roast levels and regions.
items.author: Emily Chen
items.content: Light, medium, or dark roast? Which one is more suitable for your palate? If you like acidity and fruit flavors, light-roasted coffees of African origin are for you. South American options are better for chocolate and caramel tones.

items.title: Latte Art for Beginners
items.excerpt: Methods for frothing milk at home and drawing simple patterns.
items.author: James Bond
items.content: Latte art is not just an image, it's also an indicator of the correct texture of the milk. To achieve a silky smooth 'microfoam', it's important not to overheat the milk. You can gain experience by starting with heart or leaf patterns.

items.title: Coffee and Health: Scientific Facts
items.excerpt: Research on the effects of daily coffee consumption on the body.
items.author: Dr. Linda Gray
items.content: Rich in antioxidants, coffee can protect liver health and reduce the risk of diabetes. However, excessive caffeine can affect sleep patterns. Balance is everything.

items.title: Sustainable Coffee Farming
items.excerpt: Why is environmentally friendly coffee production important?
items.author: Green World
items.content: Climate change threatens coffee plantations. 'Fair trade' and ecologically certified coffees ensure proper valuation of farmers' labor and protection of forests.

items.title: Effect of the Roasting Process on Coffee Taste
items.excerpt: The chemical transformation from green bean to cup.
items.author: Master Roaster
items.content: The Maillard reaction is the main process that determines the character of the coffee. During roasting, sugars caramelize and hundreds of different aromatic compounds are created.

items.title: Coffee Cultures Around the World
items.excerpt: Various preparation methods from Vietnam to Turkey.
items.author: Traveler Zak
items.content: Each country has its own unique rituals. Coffee with egg in Vietnam, a shot of espresso in Italy, and coffee prepared on sand in Turkey. Coffee is the common language that unites the world.

items.title: Misconceptions About Decaf Coffee
items.excerpt: About the taste and preparation methods of decaffeinated coffee.
items.author: John Doe
items.content: It's a myth that decaf coffee is tasteless. Beans decaffeinated without using chemicals with the modern 'Swiss Water' method maintain their original taste.

items.title: 5 Rules for Perfect Milk Frothing
items.excerpt: Ideal milk texture for Cappuccino and Flat White.
items.author: Barista Expert
items.content: Cold milk, a clean steam wand, and the right angle. A 'tearing' sound should be heard when you start frothing the milk, and then a vortex should be created to smooth out the texture.
